name: DROID P10
description: DROID controller. Ten pots — two large (same as P2B8) and eight small — all 0–10V / 0.0–1.0. Free assignment from the patch to any circuit parameter. Up to 16 controllers chain to a master.

controls.name: Pots 1–10 (P1.1–P1.10)
controls.description: Two large top pots + eight small pots. All identical behavior. Output 0.0 at fully CCW, 1.0 at fully CW.
controls.detail: 0.0–1.0 number range · = 0–10V if routed to an O register · use A×B+C in patch to rescale

controls.name: LAST / PARK jumper
controls.description: Three-pin jumper terminating the controller chain. Must be LAST if this P10 is the last controller, PARK or removed otherwise.
controls.detail: wrong setting: LEDs light on boot but no pot data reaches master

controls.name: LINK IN / LINK OUT
controls.description: Two 6-pin shrouded headers for chaining. LINK IN to previous controller (or master/X7), LINK OUT to next controller.
controls.detail: do not connect to the unshrouded Debug header · powered by master, no separate power

outputs.name: P1.1 (large)
outputs.description: First large pot. On first P10 in chain addressed as P1.1 in patch; on second controller in chain, P2.1; etc.

outputs.name: P1.3–P1.10 (small)
outputs.description: Eight small pots, same 0.0–1.0 range, just smaller physical knob. Harder to fine-tune but space-efficient.

behaviors.name: Pot-circuit indirection
behaviors.description: A bare P1.1 reference can be used directly as a CV, but wrapping it in a pot circuit unlocks features: notch at 0.5 (artificial detent), overlay multiple virtual pots on the same physical knob (with select), bipolar range with split halves, and 16 LED position display on the master matrix. The P10's physical travel stays constant; virtual values follow via selectat + persisted state.

behaviors.name: Multi-function overlay
behaviors.description: Declare N pot circuits all reading P1.1 but each with a different select input. Only the currently-selected virtual pot is active; others retain their last value. Classic DROID trick to get attack/decay/sustain/release on one P10 knob via a 4-way buttongroup switch. Physical rotation edits whichever virtual pot is lit.

behaviors.name: A×B+C on every parameter
behaviors.description: Wherever P1.n is used, the patch can attenuate and offset it: attack = P1.3 * 0.3 gives 0–0.3 instead of 0–1. Bipolar mapping: input = P1.1 * 2 - 1 gives −1…+1. Removes the need for external attenuverters.

behaviors.name: State persistence — virtual values
behaviors.description: The saved state in DROIDSTA.BIN records the last virtual value for each select slot, NOT the physical pot position. On power-up, virtual values are restored; the physical pot is only read when you touch it (after take-over behavior configured in the pot circuit). This prevents jumps when switching virtual menus.

behaviors.name: Chain order matters
behaviors.description: In the patch, [p10] must appear in the same order as the physical chain, starting with the controller closest to the master (or X7). Swapping order garbles incoming data — Forge visually mirrors physical chain order and offers a 'master on right' option.

behaviors.name: Ten pots, one small module
behaviors.description: At 5HP the P10 fits more knobs per HP than the P2B8 or P4B2. Trade-off: small pots are harder for fast performance edits but ideal for set-and-forget parameters (quantizer scales, algoquencer density, mixer levels) while the two large pots stay at hand for live-performance controls.

patchIdeas.name: Full ADSR + mixer on one P10
patchIdeas.patch: P1.1 attack, P1.2 decay, P1.3 sustain, P1.4 release on a contour · P1.5–P1.8 four mixer inputs with per-input attenuation done in the expression (input1 = I2 * P1.5 etc., since the mixer circuit has no separate level jacks) · two large pots stay free for live performance (filter + VCA or similar).
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* contour: gate=I1, attack=P1.1, decay=P1.2, sustain=P1.3, release=P1.4, output=O1
* mixer: input1=I2 * P1.5, input2=I3 * P1.6, input3=I4 * P1.7, input4=I5 * P1.8, output=O2

P10:

- Gate Source (Out) g> DROID (I1)
- DROID (O1) >> VCA (CV)
- DROID (O2) >> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Quantizer scale selector
patchIdeas.patch: minifonion quantizer driven from the P10 · root=P1.1 * 12 (lets the large pot sweep the 12 chromatic roots) · degree=P1.2 * 11 (selects from the 12 base scales 0..11) · seven select inputs mapped to small pots so you can dial in custom chord-tone sets (select1 = root, select3 = 3rd, select5 = 5th, etc.) · P1.10 = transpose in volts · full scale-control panel inside 5HP.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* minifonion: input=I1, root=P1.1 * 12, degree=P1.2 * 11, select1=P1.3, select3=P1.4, select5=P1.5, select7=P1.6, select9=P1.7, select11=P1.8, select13=P1.9, transpose=P1.10, output=O1

P10:

- Sequencer (CV) p> DROID (I1)
- DROID (O1) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Eight-step sequencer
patchIdeas.patch: sequencer circuit with pitch1..pitch8 tied to P1.1..P1.8 · clock on I1 · P1.9 * 8 scaled into stages to set active length (1–8 steps) · P1.10 routed to gatelength for variable gate shape · pitchoutput on O1 to VCO, gateoutput on O2 to envelope · pots directly set step voltages (not quantized — add a minifonion after O1 if you want musical pitches).
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* sequencer: clock=I1, pitch1=P1.1, pitch2=P1.2, pitch3=P1.3, pitch4=P1.4, pitch5=P1.5, pitch6=P1.6, pitch7=P1.7, pitch8=P1.8, stages=P1.9 * 8, gatelength=P1.10, pitchoutput=O1, gateoutput=O2

P10:

- Clock (Out) c> DROID (I1)
- DROID (O1) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- DROID (O2) g> VCA Env (Gate)

patchIdeas.name: Overlay activity with select
patchIdeas.patch: Two algoquencer tracks sharing one P1.1 · buttongroup on P2B8 (B2.1/B2.2) lights L2.1/L2.2 · pot circuit 1: select=L2.1 → output _A1 · pot circuit 2: select=L2.2 → output _A2 · turning P1.1 edits only the currently-lit track's activity · other virtual value frozen (DROIDSTA.BIN).
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* buttongroup: button1=B2.1, button2=B2.2, led1=L2.1, led2=L2.2
* pot: pot=P1.1, select=L2.1, output=_A1
* pot: pot=P1.1, select=L2.2, output=_A2
* algoquencer: clock=I1, activity=_A1, trigger=O1
* algoquencer: clock=I1, activity=_A2, trigger=O2

P10:

P2B8:

- Clock (Out) c> DROID (I1)
- DROID (O1) t> Drum 1 (Trig)
- DROID (O2) t> Drum 2 (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Live modulation matrix
patchIdeas.patch: Ten independent LFOs, one per pot · each pot sets the rate of its lfo · outputs summed pairwise into mixers · sends modulation to O1..O5 · P10 becomes a live modulation console where each knob sweeps an LFO rate from slow drift to audio-rate · perfect for drones, generative textures.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* lfo: hz=P1.1, sine=O1
* lfo: hz=P1.2, sine=O2
* lfo: hz=P1.3, triangle=O3
* lfo: hz=P1.4, triangle=O4
* lfo: hz=P1.5, square=O5

P10:

Filter:

VCA:

Pan:

FX:

Drone:

- DROID (O1) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- DROID (O2) >> VCA (CV)
- DROID (O3) >> Pan (CV)
- DROID (O4) >> FX (Time)
- DROID (O5) >> Drone (PWM)

patchIdeas.name: Bipolar offsets × 10
patchIdeas.patch: Ten copy circuits, each doing its attenuation + offset in the input expression (the copy circuit has no multiplier/offset jacks — scaling is done with the standard A*B+C syntax): input = P1.n * 2 - 1 gives a -1…+1 bipolar offset voltage · route to O1..O8 (the two large pots can feed internal cables for extra destinations) · manual CV offset bank in 5HP.
patchIdeas.diagram:
DROID:
* copy: input=P1.1 * 2 - 1, output=O1
* copy: input=P1.2 * 2 - 1, output=O2
* copy: input=P1.3 * 2 - 1, output=O3
* copy: input=P1.4 * 2 - 1, output=O4
* copy: input=P1.5 * 2 - 1, output=O5
* copy: input=P1.6 * 2 - 1, output=O6
* copy: input=P1.7 * 2 - 1, output=O7
* copy: input=P1.8 * 2 - 1, output=O8

P10:

- DROID (O1) >> Destination 1 (CV)
- DROID (O2) >> Destination 2 (CV)
